---
# CHAPTER TEMPLATE — copy this file, rename it, and fill in the content.
# Replace all <PLACEHOLDER> tags. Remove this comment block.
#
# Filename convention:  parts/<NN-part-slug>/<MM-chapter-slug>.qmd
# Label convention:     sec-<slug>  (for cross-referencing with @sec-<slug>)
title: "<Module number>. <Chapter title>"
author: Robert Waddy
date: last-modified
---

```{=html}
<div class="chapter-header-meta">
  <strong>Part N — Part title</strong> &middot; Module MN &middot; <em>Tier: Core + Going deeper</em>
</div>
```

<!-- One-sentence module summary for orientation. -->
*<Module summary: what problem this solves and why it matters experimentally.>*

---

## Core {#sec-<slug>-core}

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" icon="false"}
### Core — <Chapter title>

<!-- Self-contained examinable material. -->
<!-- Target: ~4 pages in the PDF. -->

<Content here.>

:::

---

## Going deeper {#sec-<slug>-deeper}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal" icon="false"}
### Going deeper

<!-- MSc/PhD-level extensions, derivations, edge cases. -->

<Content here.>

:::

---

## Bench {#sec-<slug>-bench}

::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal" icon="false"}
### Bench Notes for this module

The following Bench Notes expand on specific tools or design rules introduced above:

- [Bench Note: <Title>](<path-to-bench-note>.qmd)

:::

---

## Key references {#sec-<slug>-refs}

::: {.callout-important appearance="minimal" icon="false"}
### Key references

- <Primary reference> [@<key>]
- <Secondary reference> [@<key>]

:::

---

## Exercises {#sec-<slug>-exercises .unnumbered}

1. <Exercise 1.>
2. <Exercise 2.>
